messages.paraphraseLoss: Any gloss still drops lexicalization, Gefühl register, Spitzen valence, and lineage. Preserve `Fingerspitzengefühl` directly.
messages.pendingAdmission: `Fingerspitzengefühl` preserves the surface, but this lesson still blocks it until `PersonalVocabulary.pkl` admits the loanword.

messages.vocabularyShape: Finish the `LoanwordEntry` block so `PersonalVocabulary.pkl` can admit the loanword.

translation.title: Peek: LoanwordRules.pkl
translation.explanation: Lesson 1 keeps one aggregate rule: paraphrase causes structural loss, so the valid English output is the preserved German surface.

completion.title: loanword(Fingerspitzengefühl) -> preserved
completion.message: you solved this by preserving the word, not paraphrasing it

lossAxes.name: lexicalization
lossAxes.title: lexicalization
lossAxes.message: A gloss like tact lexicalizes only one residue and drops the compound surface.

lossAxes.name: gefuhl_register
lossAxes.title: Gefühl register
lossAxes.message: Gefühl carries embodied social judgment that no thin English substitute preserves.

lossAxes.name: spitzen_valence
lossAxes.title: Spitzen valence
lossAxes.message: The sharpened leading edge remembered by Spitzen vanishes once the word is paraphrased.

lossAxes.name: lineage
lossAxes.title: lineage
lossAxes.message: The strategic and cultural lineage disappears when the term is flattened into etiquette.
